Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 303 [quitclaim][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

u₂-il₃-ti₃ ša₂ KU₃.BABBAR <m>ni-din!-<tu₄>—dNA₃ DUMU

(o 1) The contract-tablet of the silver (from) Nidintu-Nabu, son of Ahšunu, son of Laqip, which Ubar, son of Anu-ahhe-iqiša, has taken over on account of the house of Ahšunu, is paid.

o 22

ša₂ mŠEŠ-šu₂-nu DUMU ša₂ mla-qip

o 33

ša₂ ina muḫ-ḫi mu₂-bar DUMU ša₂

o 44

md60—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—BAša₂ ša₂ ina E₂

o 55

mŠEŠ-šu₂-nu te-la-ʾ

o 66

e-ṭi₅-ra!-tu₄ ši-na!

Reverse
r 1r 1

lu₂mu-kin₇ md60—NUMUN—MU

(r 1) Witnesses: Anu-zer-iddin, son of Anu-ahhe-iddin; Anu-zer-lišir, son of Anu-kaṣir; Anu-belšunu, son of Nidinti-Anu; Šamaš-iqiša, son of Iddin-Nabu.

r 22

DUMU ša₂ md60—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—MU

r 33

md60—NUMUN—GIŠ DUMU ša₂ md60—KAD₂

r 44

md60—EN-šu₂-nu DUMU ša₂ mni-din-ti—d60

r 55

mdUTU—BAša₂ DUMU ša₂ mMU—dNA₃

r 66

mŠEŠ-šu₂-nu lu₂UMBISAG DUMU ša₂ mla-qip

(r 6) Ahšunu, the scribe, son of Laqip. At Uruk, the month Addaru, 18th day, 8th year <of PN, king of> all the lands.

r 77

UNUGki itiŠE U₄ 18-KAM₂

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

MU 8-KAM₂ KUR.KUR

(t.e. 1) All the earlier credits [...]

t.e. 22

⸢IGItu₄⸣ ra-šu-tu gab-bi [...]

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

[ṣu?-pur?] ⸢ša₂ mŠEŠ⸣-šu₂-nu

(b.e. 1) [Nail] of Ahšunu.
